 {"id":"bd-28db","title":"Add 'bd status' command for issue database overview","description":"Implement a bd status command that provides a quick snapshot of the issue database state, similar to how git status shows working tree state.

Expected output: Show summary including counts by state (open, in-progress, blocked, closed), recent activity (last 7 days), and quick overview without needing multiple queries.

Example output showing issue counts, recent activity stats, and pointer to bd list for details.

Proposed options: --all (show all issues), --assigned (show issues assigned to current user), --json (JSON format output)

Use cases: Quick project health check, onboarding for new contributors, integration with shell prompts or CI/CD, daily standup reference","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-02T17:25:59.203549-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:54:00.205191-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T17:54:00.205191-08:00","close_reason":"Already implemented - bd status shows summary and activity"}

-{"id":"bd-2l03","title":"Implement await type handlers (gh:run, gh:pr, timer, human, mail)","description":"Implement condition checking for each await type.

## Handlers Needed
- gh:run:<id> - Check GitHub Actions run status via gh CLI
- gh:pr:<id> - Check PR merged/closed status via gh CLI  
- timer:<duration> - Simple elapsed time check
- human:<prompt> - Check for human approval (via mail?)
- mail:<pattern> - Check for mail matching pattern

## Implementation Location
This is Deacon logic, so likely in Gas Town (gt) not beads.

## Interface
```go
type AwaitHandler interface {
    Check(awaitID string) (completed bool, result string, err error)
}
```","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:38.492837-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:38.492837-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2l03","depends_on_id":"bd-udsi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:52.990746-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-2l03","depends_on_id":"bd-is6m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:56.510792-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

## Changes
- Add metadata column to dependencies table
- Add thread_id column for conversation grouping
- Remove redundant Issue fields: replies_to, relates_to, duplicate_of, superseded_by
- Update all code to use dependencies API
- Migration script for existing data
- JSONL format change (breaking)

 {"id":"bd-396j","title":"GetBlockedIssues shows external deps as blocking even when satisfied","description":"GetBlockedIssues (ready.go:385-493) shows external:* refs in the blocked_by list but doesn't check if they're actually satisfied using CheckExternalDep.

This can be confusing - an issue shows as blocked by external:project:capability even if that capability has been shipped (closed issue with provides: label exists).

Options:
1. Call CheckExternalDep for each external ref and filter satisfied ones from blocked_by
2. Add a note in output indicating external deps need lazy resolution
3. Document this is expected behavior (bd blocked shows all deps, bd ready shows resolved state)

Related: GetReadyWork correctly filters by external deps, but GetBlockedIssues doesn't.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T23:45:05.286304-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T21:48:38.086451-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T21:48:38.086451-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented filterBlockedByExternalDeps to check external deps satisfaction and filter them from BlockedBy lists","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-396j","depends_on_id":"bd-zmmy","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-21T23:45:05.286971-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3bsz","title":"gt mail send: support reading message body from stdin","description":"Currently gt mail send -m requires the message as a command-line argument, which causes shell escaping issues with backticks, quotes, and special characters.

Add support for reading message body from stdin:
- gt mail send addr -s 'Subject' --stdin  # Read body from stdin
- echo 'body' | gt mail send addr -s 'Subject' -m -  # Convention: -m - means stdin

This would allow:
cat <<'EOF' | gt mail send addr -s 'Subject' --stdin
Message with `backticks` and 'quotes' safely
EOF

Without this, agents struggle to send handoff messages containing code snippets.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-23T03:21:39.496208-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T03:21:39.496208-08:00"}
+{"id":"bd-3bsz","title":"gt mail send: support reading message body from stdin","description":"Currently gt mail send -m requires the message as a command-line argument, which causes shell escaping issues with backticks, quotes, and special characters.

Add support for reading message body from stdin:
- gt mail send addr -s 'Subject' --stdin  # Read body from stdin
- echo 'body' | gt mail send addr -s 'Subject' -m -  # Convention: -m - means stdin

This would allow:
cat <<'EOF' | gt mail send addr -s 'Subject' --stdin
Message with `backticks` and 'quotes' safely
EOF

Without this, agents struggle to send handoff messages containing code snippets.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-23T03:21:39.496208-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T12:19:44.443554-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T12:19:44.443554-08:00","close_reason":"Moved to gastown: gt-rw2z"}

 {"id":"bd-iq19","title":"Distill: promote ad-hoc epic to proto","description":"Extract a reusable proto from an existing ad-hoc epic.

COMMAND: bd mol distill <epic-id> [--as <proto-name>]

BEHAVIOR:
- Clone the epic and all children as a new proto
- Set is_template=true on all cloned issues
- Replace concrete values with {{variable}} placeholders (interactive or --var flags)
- Add to proto catalog

FLAGS:
- --as NAME: Custom proto ID (default: proto-<epic-id>)
- --var field=placeholder: Replace value with variable placeholder
- --interactive: Prompt for each field that looks parameterizable
- --dry-run: Preview the proto structure

EXAMPLE:
  bd mol distill bd-o5xe --as proto-feature-workflow \
    --var title=feature_name \
    --var assignee=worker

USE CASES:
- Team develops good workflow organically, wants to reuse it
- Capture tribal knowledge as executable templates
- Create starting point for similar future work

The reverse of spawn: instead of proto → molecule, it's molecule → proto.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T01:05:07.953538-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T10:31:56.814246-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T10:31:56.814246-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented distill command in mol.go","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iq19","depends_on_id":"bd-o5xe","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T01:05:16.495774-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-iq19","depends_on_id":"bd-rnnr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T01:05:16.560404-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-iq7n","title":"Audit and fix JSONL filename mismatches across all repo clones","description":"## Problem

Multiple clones of repos are configured with different JSONL filenames (issues.jsonl vs beads.jsonl), causing:
1. JSONL files to be resurrected after deletion (one clone pushes issues.jsonl, another pushes beads.jsonl)
2. Agents unable to see issues filed by other agents after sync
3. Merge conflicts and data inconsistencies

## Root Cause

When repos were "bd doctored" or initialized at different times, some got issues.jsonl (old default) and others got beads.jsonl (Beads repo specific). These clones push their respective files, creating duplicates.

## Task

Scan all repo clones under ~/src/ (1-2 levels deep) and standardize their JSONL configuration.

### Step 1: Find all beads-enabled repos

```bash
# Find all directories named 'beads' at levels 1-2 under ~/src/
find ~/src -maxdepth 2 -type d -name beads
```

### Step 2: For each repo found, check configuration

For each directory from Step 1, check:
- Does `.beads/metadata.json` exist?
- What is the `jsonl_export` value?
- What JSONL files actually exist in `.beads/`?
- Are there multiple JSONL files (problem!)?

### Step 3: Create audit report

Generate a report showing:
```
Repo Path                           | Config        | Actual Files           | Status
----------------------------------- | ------------- | ---------------------- | --------
~/src/beads                         | beads.jsonl   | beads.jsonl            | OK
~/src/dave/beads                    | issues.jsonl  | issues.jsonl           | MISMATCH
~/src/emma/beads                    | issues.jsonl  | issues.jsonl, beads.jsonl | DUPLICATE!
```

### Step 4: Determine canonical name for each repo

For repos that are the SAME git repository (check `git remote -v`):
- Group them together
- Determine which JSONL filename should be canonical (majority wins, or beads.jsonl for the beads repo itself)
- List which clones need to be updated

### Step 5: Generate fix script

Create a script that for each mismatched clone:
1. Updates `.beads/metadata.json` to use the canonical name
2. If JSONL file needs renaming: `git mv .beads/old.jsonl .beads/new.jsonl`
3. Removes any duplicate JSONL files: `git rm .beads/duplicate.jsonl`
4. Commits the change
5. Syncs: `bd sync`

### Expected Output

1. Audit report showing all repos and their config status
2. List of repos grouped by git remote (same repository)
3. Fix script or manual instructions for standardizing each repo
4. Verification that after fixes, all clones of the same repo use the same JSONL filename

### Edge Cases

- Handle repos without metadata.json (use default discovery)
- Handle repos with no git remote (standalone/local)
- Handle repos that are not git repositories
- Don't modify repos with uncommitted changes (warn instead)

### Success Criteria

- All clones of the same git repository use the same JSONL filename
- No duplicate JSONL files in any repo
- All configurations documented in metadata.json
- bd doctor passes on all repos","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-21T23:58:35.044762-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T23:13:40.531403-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T16:50:59.510972-08:00"}
-{"id":"bd-is6m","title":"Add gate checking to Deacon patrol loop","description":"Integrate gate checking into Deacon's patrol cycle.

## Patrol Integration
```go
func (d *Deacon) checkGates(ctx context.Context) {
    gates, _ := d.store.ListOpenGates(ctx)
    
    for _, gate := range gates {
        // Check timeout
        if time.Since(gate.CreatedAt) > gate.Timeout {
            d.notifyWaiters(gate, "timeout")
            d.closeGate(gate, "timed out")
            continue
        }
        
        // Check condition
        if d.checkCondition(gate.AwaitType, gate.AwaitID) {
            d.notifyWaiters(gate, "cleared")
            d.closeGate(gate, "condition met")
        }
    }
}
```

## Note
This task is in Gas Town (gt), not beads. +{"id":"bd-is6m","title":"Add gate checking to Deacon patrol loop","description":"Integrate gate checking into Deacon's patrol cycle.

## Patrol Integration
```go
func (d *Deacon) checkGates(ctx context.Context) {
    gates, _ := d.store.ListOpenGates(ctx)
    
    for _, gate := range gates {
        // Check timeout
        if time.Since(gate.CreatedAt) > gate.Timeout {
            d.notifyWaiters(gate, "timeout")
            d.closeGate(gate, "timed out")
            continue
        }
        
        // Check condition
        if d.checkCondition(gate.AwaitType, gate.AwaitID) {
            d.notifyWaiters(gate, "cleared")
            d.closeGate(gate, "condition met")
        }
    }
}
```

## Note
This task is in Gas Town (gt), not beads. May need to be moved there.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:36.839709-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T12:19:44.204647-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T12:19:44.204647-08:00","close_reason":"Moved to gastown: gt-dh65","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-is6m","depends_on_id":"bd-udsi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:52.909253-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-is6m","depends_on_id":"bd-u66e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:56.428084-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-iw4z","title":"Compound visualization in bd mol show","description":"Enhance bd mol show to display compound structure.

ENHANCEMENTS:
- Show constituent protos and how they're bonded
- Display bond type (sequential/parallel) between components
- Indicate attachment points
- Show combined variable requirements across all protos

EXAMPLE OUTPUT:

  Compound: proto-feature-with-tests
  Bonded from:
    └─ proto-feature (root)
       └─ proto-testing (sequential, after completion)
  
  Variables: {{name}}, {{version}}, {{test_suite}}
  
  Structure:
    proto-feature-with-tests
    ├─ Design feature {{name}}
    ├─ Implement core
    ├─ Write unit tests      ← from proto-testing
    └─ Run test suite {{test_suite}}  ← from proto-testing","status":"deferred","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T00:59:26.71318-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T11:12:44.012871-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iw4z","depends_on_id":"bd-o5xe","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T00:59:51.500865-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-iw4z","depends_on_id":"bd-rnnr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T00:59:51.891643-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-j0tr","title":"Phase 1.3: Basic TOON read/write operations","description":"Add basic TOON read/write operations to bdt executable. Implement create, list, and show commands that use the internal/toon package for encoding/decoding to TOON format.

## Subtasks
1. Implement bdt create command - Create issues and serialize to TOON format
2. Implement bdt list command - Read issues.toon and display all issues
3. Implement bdt show command - Display single issue by ID
4. Add file I/O operations for issues.toon
5. Integrate internal/toon package (EncodeTOON, DecodeJSON)
6. Write tests for create, list, show operations

## Files to Create/Modify
- cmd/bdt/create.go - Create command
- cmd/bdt/list.go - List command  
- cmd/bdt/show.go - Show command
- cmd/bdt/storage.go - File I/O helper

## Success Criteria
- bdt create "Issue title" creates and saves to issues.toon
- bdt list displays all issues in human-readable format
- bdt list --json shows JSON output
- bdt show <id> displays single issue
- Issues round-trip correctly: create → list → show
- All tests passing with >80% coverage","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T12:59:54.270296918-07:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T13:09:00.196045685-07:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T13:09:00.196045685-07:00"}
 {"id":"bd-j3il","title":"Add bd reset command for clean slate restart","description":"Implement a command to reset beads to a clean starting state.

**Context:** GitHub issue #479 - users sometimes get beads into an invalid state after updates, and there's no clean way to start fresh. The git backup/restore mechanism that protects against accidental deletion also makes it hard to intentionally reset.

**Current workaround** (from maphew):
```bash
bd daemons killall
git rm .beads/*.jsonl
git commit -m 'remove old issues'
rm .beads/*
bd init
bd onboard
```

**Desired:** A proper `bd reset` command that handles this cleanly and safely.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-13T08:41:34.956552+11:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T08:43:49.970591+11:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T08:43:49.970591+11:00"}

 {"id":"bd-yck","title":"Fix checkExistingBeadsData to be worktree-aware","description":"The checkExistingBeadsData function in cmd/bd/init.go checks for .beads in the current working directory, but for worktrees it should check the main repository root instead. This prevents proper worktree compatibility.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:48:32.082776345-07:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:48:32.082776345-07:00"}
 {"id":"bd-ykd9","title":"Add bd doctor --fix flag to automatically repair issues","description":"Implement a --fix flag for bd doctor that can automatically repair detected issues.

Requirements:
- Add --fix flag to bd doctor command
- Show all fixable issues and prompt for confirmation before applying fixes
- Organize fix implementations under doctor/fix/<type_of_fix>.go
- Each fix type should have its own file (e.g., doctor/fix/hooks.go, doctor/fix/sync.go)
- Display what will be fixed and ask user to confirm (Y/n) before proceeding
- Support fixing issues like:
  - Missing or broken git hooks
  - Sync problems with remote
  - File permission issues
  - Any other auto-repairable issues doctor detects

Implementation notes:
- Maintain separation between detection (existing doctor code) and repair (new fix code)
- Each fix should be idempotent and safe to run multiple times
- Provide clear output about what was fixed
- Log any fixes that fail with actionable error messages","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-14T18:17:48.411264-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-14T18:17:58.88609-08:00"}
-{"id":"bd-ykqu","title":"Add gate timeout tracking and notification","description":"Implement timeout and notification logic for gates.

## Timeout Behavior
1. Gate created with timeout (e.g., 30m)
2. Deacon tracks elapsed time during patrol
3. If timeout reached:
   - Notify all waiters: "Gate timed out"
   - Close gate with timeout reason
   - Waiter can retry, escalate, or fail gracefully

## Notification
- Use gt mail send to notify waiters
- Include gate ID, await type, and reason in message
- Support multiple waiters notification

## Escalation Path
- Witness sees stuck worker, nudges them
- Worker can escalate to human if needed","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:40.1825-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:40.1825-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ykqu","depends_on_id":"bd-udsi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:53.072862-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-ykqu","depends_on_id":"bd-is6m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:56.595085-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"bd-ykqu","title":"Add gate timeout tracking and notification","description":"Implement timeout and notification logic for gates.

## Timeout Behavior
1. Gate created with timeout (e.g., 30m)
2. Deacon tracks elapsed time during patrol
3. If timeout reached:
   - Notify all waiters: "Gate timed out"
   - Close gate with timeout reason
   - Waiter can retry, escalate, or fail gracefully

## Notification
- Use gt mail send to notify waiters
- Include gate ID, await type, and reason in message
- Support multiple waiters notification

## Escalation Path
- Witness sees stuck worker, nudges them
- Worker can escalate to human if needed","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:40.1825-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T12:19:44.362527-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T12:19:44.362527-08:00","close_reason":"Moved to gastown: gt-fqcz","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ykqu","depends_on_id":"bd-udsi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:53.072862-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-ykqu","depends_on_id":"bd-is6m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:56.595085-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-yqhh","title":"bd list --parent: filter by parent issue","description":"Add --parent flag to bd list to filter issues by parent.

Example:
```bash
bd list --parent=gt-h5n --status=open
```

Would show all open children of gt-h5n.

Useful for:
- Checking epic progress
- Finding swarmable work within an epic
- Molecule step listing","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-23T01:51:26.830952-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T02:10:12.909803-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T02:10:12.909803-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented --parent flag for bd list.
